root_cause_hypotheses.title: Workflow snapshot / trigger mismatch at event time
root_cause_hypotheses.explanation: Runs exist whose head SHA's workflow file could not be retrieved (yaml not found). If a run was created while the workflow file was being added/renamed/removed, GitHub can record the run but not schedule jobs (jobs=0). This matches the observed runs whose head_sha lacked a saved workflow.

root_cause_hypotheses.title: Transient GitHub Actions backend job-scheduling/log retention failure
root_cause_hypotheses.explanation: Some runs show 404 when retrieving logs while a minimal diagnostic workflow succeeded earlier. This pattern indicates occasional platform-side failures where the run record exists but job scheduling or log packaging fails, producing jobs=0 and logs 404.

recommended_minimal_patches.title: Add an early diagnostic job that always runs and uploads an artifact
recommended_minimal_patches.summary: Create a small first job `selfheal-diagnostic` that runs immediately, prints the `github.sha`/`github.event` and uploads a small artifact. This provides an auditable, minimal indicator that jobs were scheduled and logs collected even when the main job fails to run.
recommended_minimal_patches.yaml_snippet:
jobs:
  selfheal-diagnostic:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: echo "diag: ${{ github.sha }} ${{ github.event_name }}" > diag.txt
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: selfheal-diagnostic-${{ github.sha }}
          path: diag.txt
recommended_minimal_patches.risk: Low (adds small job and artifact storage)
rollout_steps: Add the job to the top of the workflow (before long E2E steps).
Monitor for presence of diagnostic artifacts on subsequent runs to confirm job scheduling.

recommended_minimal_patches.title: Explicitly declare `permissions` required
recommended_minimal_patches.summary: Add an explicit `permissions:` block to avoid relying on defaults and to make the workflow's required tokens explicit (reduces failure modes caused by permissions drift).
rollout_steps: Verify diagnostic job can still upload artifact and any GH CLI steps continue to work.

recommended_minimal_patches.title: Add `concurrency` to avoid overlapping runs and race conditions
recommended_minimal_patches.summary: Declare `concurrency` to cancel overlapping self-heal runs and avoid in-flight workflow updates interfering with triggers.
rollout_steps: Observe behavior over several scheduled runs to ensure expected cancellations.

recommended_minimal_patches.title: Upload the workflow file as an artifact for forensic debugging
recommended_minimal_patches.summary: Add a step that saves the resolved workflow YAML (the file in .github/workflows) and uploads it as an artifact; this makes it straightforward to compare the workflow that actually ran with repository history when investigating jobs=0.
recommended_minimal_patches.yaml_snippet:
- name: Save workflow YAML
  run: cp .github/workflows/triage-dashboard-e2e-selfheal.yml ./workflow-snapshot.yml || true
- uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: selfheal-workflow-snapshot-${{ github.sha }}
    path: workflow-snapshot.yml
rollout_steps: Add step early in the job to capture the workflow file.
Use artifacts during incident investigations to correlate run metadata to workflow content.

notes: Patches are intentionally minimal and forensic-first: make failures visible, reduce race conditions, and provide evidence for GitHub Support. If jobs=0 persists after these patches, collect failing run ids and open a GitHub Support ticket including the diagnostic artifacts and workflow snapshots.
